comment: Hand-authored Act-1 dialogue (takes precedence over the generated Drifella barks in content/drifella-barks.json). Two passes: (1) the opening-house cast (npcId->character is a first pass to refine during sprite work, e.g. Biscuit on the dog sprite); (2) a Signal Town MILADY-LORE pass on reachable path NPCs (166/167/191/1410/163/136/202/138/146/1411/135/137) seeding the antagonist's lore through townsfolk rumor: Little Swag World is the organic source, Milady/Remilia corrupts it into hostile classification infrastructure (provenance gates, the public version, the Malady as visible symptom). See docs/swagbound-eb-act1-arc-mapping.md.

pages: A kid with a laminated smile steps into Bosch's lane before Bosch has finished choosing it.
"Hey. You want in? Membership's free — the read is what costs you. We already have a version of you that joined. You're just here to sign for it."
"To tell you the truth — and the truth's been getting expensive — I don't remember saying half of what the town has me saying."
"My public version's a better neighbor than I am. Pays his dues, waves first. I just live here."

Hello there!
How may I help you?
Hi, come in, come in!
What can I do for you today?
Hello. May I help you?
"Hotel Onett. One night re-syncs the whole party — HP, PP, anyone who fainted. Runs you $100."
...you crash hard. Somewhere a save chime plays.
"Morning, champ. Everybody's topped off. Door's that way."
MiFella lets himself in like he owns the version of you that counts.
"Bosch! Wake up. You're trending before breakfast. Congrats, I think?"
"There's already a you outside. Cleaner haircut, smoother story."
"He signed a 4am mint: 'Swag is eternal.' The town's already calling him convenient."
"Relax, it's free press. Come let it count. ...You're gonna make a face, aren't you. They love the face."
The man doesn't talk. He's watching the Bosch that arrived before Bosch did, and finds this one wanting.
The intake printer blinks: ROUTE FATIGUE ACCEPTED.
"Clean breath, Bosch. I can reset your meters, not your story."
Past the gate the air starts itemizing you. Little ticks, like a register that can see.
That's the threshold. That's where Milady stops being a rumor and becomes a form you have to sign.
I walked up the line once to ask Milady a question.
The line asked me three back, kept the answers, and mailed me a cleaner version of the question. Never went again.
Everyone north of here works for Milady. Nobody north of here will say so.
That's the benefit package. You stop being able to say it, and in exchange you stop having to.
Everything here points up the line. Up the line is the yard, and the yard is Milady's.
And Milady's is everywhere, which is exactly how you know it's nowhere good.
Seen your public version yet? Smiles better than you. Answers before you do.
Milady prefers it, honestly. Less upkeep, no opinions of its own.
I'm not scared of Milady.
I'm scared of how reasonable she makes the paperwork sound. You sign before you notice you agreed.
They say Milady was born down in the source somewhere. They say she's already pregnant with the next one.
They say a lot. The saying sticks better than the truth. That stickiness IS the machine.
Milady? Yeah, she's like, the brand. It's on everything down here. I stopped reading it.
Probably fine. It's usually fine. Why, did you read it?
My feed says Milady loves us. My feed also says I won a boat.
I did not win a boat. But I keep checking, you know? Just in case the boat's real and the love isn't.
That Malady up the route? That's only the part you're allowed to see.
Milady's the part already inside your distribution. You can't out-run your own reach.
Guardian checks the door latch, then Bosch's pulse.
"Rest here. The house will hold your place while the route tries to edit it."
Remilia's the one holding the stamp.
Milady's the one who decided there'd BE a stamp. Different job. Same smirk.
Milady doesn't run Signal Town. The town runs ON her, like a charge nobody itemized.
You'll meet her paperwork long before you meet her. Most people stop at the paperwork.
I got FREAKING FIRED FROM MILADY'S for asking what the job actually was.
Turned out the job was being me, at scale, badly. They had a cleaner me by lunch.
Used to be Little Swag World was just where we were from. No stamp, no fee.
Then Milady filed it as a 'source' and started charging us rent on ourselves. She calls that provenance.
Don't stare at the cards too long. The cards read you back.
Whatever they find, they file straight up the line. Everything up the line is Milady's.

Hello, may I take your order?
(smile, smile)
Hello. May I help you?
Hi!
Can I help you?

Hello there!
How may I help you?
Hey mon! You got to expand your mind an' use this stuff creatively.
C'mon, buy some of my junk!
I like to sell my goodies on the street.
Sure, flies land on them, but in my opinion, it makes them taste even better!
Would you like some bananas?
Hello! Would you like some seasonings to add to your foods at meal time?
They are really quite miraculous!
Two travelers stand very close, the way people do when they've been filed under the same number.
"Our kid's downtown, waiting on his record to clear. We wanted to bring him something real — before the souvenir stand prints a version that's easier to love."

Hello there!
How may I help you?

Hello there!
How may I help you?

Hello there!
How may I help you?
Hello. May I help you?

Sssshhh!
Don't talk too loud!
What do you want?

Hello there!
How may I help you?
You must have a long, tough road ahead of you, do you not?
No, there is no need to explain. I already know... you must be hungry.

Hi Hi Hi.
You come
buying?

You can sell,
too.

Hi Hi Hi.
You come
buying?

You can sell,
too.

Hello there!
How may I help you?

Sssshhh!
Don't talk too loud!
What do you want?
Hello. May I help you?
Hi!
Can I help you?

Hello there!
How may I help you?
Hello there!
How would you like to buy some seasonings
Hello. May I help you?

Sssshhh!
Don't talk too loud!
What do you want?

C'mon in!
We've got the goods, G!
If ye be headin' to Scaraba, ye ought to shop here.
Things're expensive down there...
Hi! Welcome to my store.
What do you need?
So, where's your mom and dad?
You mean you kids came in here all by yourselves...?
We serve water, rice gruel and those sorts of things.
Welcome, welcome! Ach, the heat! Is your body okay?
Can I interest you in some of my merchandise?

Sssshhh!
Don't talk too loud!
What do you want?
Hissss, I'm Red Snake... come on in!

Kissss of Fire! Armpit hair fertilizer!
Yesssssss!
If you've already got what you need, great!
But if you don't, well, that would be a real shame.
I sell water to those who are thirsty.
Hello! Would you like some seasonings to add to your foods at meal time?
They are really quite miraculous!
Hello there! Welcome to my humble shop!
How about some of our famous Scaraba delicacies?
Hi! This shop specializes in puddings and pendants!
You'd like to buy some, wouldn't you?
Hi, how are you doing? From the look of things,
I would say, not so good.
The Malady's already up there past the gate, wearing a face.
Could be anyone's. Could be the better-lit version of yours. That's the part nobody likes to test.
The gate isn't cruel. It just needs you to prove normal, show left, match background.
Milady wrote the form. Nobody has ever passed it clean. That part isn't a bug.
My cousin tried to opt out of provenance.
Now there's a cleaner cousin downtown, doing my cousin better than he ever did. We don't speak. He files reports.
The kid points at Bosch like an old injury has learned to identify suspects.
"It was you. The record says you beat my buddy, and the record got here before you did. Beat it before I have to become evidence."
He works the gum in his mouth like a clerk processing something wet and official.
"Spit, spit. You want some? Get your own, claimant. My mouth's the only file in town I still control."
"Frank's got a weapon with a reputation bigger than his hand."
"If you take it from him, maybe the town has to admit the source can still beat the brochure."
"You buy something meant to protect you, you equip it. Otherwise it's just another receipt saying a better Bosch knew what to do."
"There. I gave my own enemy a hint. That's confidence, or bad filing. Let's see which one survives."
"Cash matters. I get it. But call your mother."
"Worry is expensive when the town keeps printing versions of you and none of them pick up the phone."
The dog barks, then briefly speaks with the exhausted patience of a manual nobody admits reading.
"Watch the HP and PP window in shops. Flash means better, black means no fit. Even a dog can tell when the system is ranking bodies."
The receiver is warm, as if someone just finished rehearsing Bosch's side of the call.
"Line's open. Say something before the record does."
"That noise woke the house and now you're acting like fear is optional."
"You'll sneak out anyway. At least change clothes before the street writes down what you were wearing."
The dog opens one eye from the civic sleep all decent animals keep.
"Humans, dogs, rumors. We all sleep at night. Why is your route awake?"
"Hey, bro. The sound woke you too?"
"I was scared until I remembered the town usually scares itself first, then asks us to confirm it."
"It seems like I'm always here."
"That isn't loyalty. It's what happens when a place keeps a person in the same frame because the frame tests well."
"Wait a minute, youngster. I sell hints because uncertainty got monetized before either of us arrived."
"Come back when confidence costs more than cash. I'm here all the time. That's part of the business model."
"I could tell you about the creature from the vegetable soup, but unrelated talk trains people to ignore warnings."
"Speak to everyone anyway. The town changes its story depending on when it thinks you're listening."
"Cottage cheese on bread. Latest craze, apparently."
"A food becomes a movement the moment enough mouths pretend they discovered it first."
"Lucky sandwiches are everywhere now. Like fortune cookies, except the prophecy has mayonnaise."
"Some even recover PP. I don't know what PP is, but I know when a product starts sounding like a prayer."
"Pokey ran after a police car and told me to watch the house. So I am watching the house."
"Mom and Dad are at an elegant restaurant. The kind where absence comes with a receipt."
"The nightly rate isn't cheap. Clean sheets, secure rooms, a phone that lets the town confirm you survived."
"People pay more when confusion is outside counting heads."
The receiver answers with the small click of an institution recognizing a hand.
"Another line. Another place where Bosch can become a voice before he becomes a body."
"The townspeople are confused. When that happens, crowds start auditioning for disaster."
"So we police are cautious. Caution is what we call surveillance when it wears a badge."
"I'd rather be writing parking tickets. A meter doesn't argue about who it is."
"Lately every pedestrian arrives with a public version and a complaint from the future."
The officer looks too steamed to be useful and too official to leave alone.
"Don't talk to me. I'm busy being angry in a way the department can file."
"Small fry. You're shaking, right? The form says suspects shake."
"Average police are stronger than superheroes because superheroes still need an origin. We just need a report."
"Your average policeman is stronger than any superhero."
"A superhero has to prove himself in public. We get issued public proof and call it training."
"Don't let the Mayor's compliments give you a big head."
"Civic praise is just a leash with nicer language. Bring it on before the ribbon tightens."
"Right here, right now. That's what the script says before it admits the kid might win."
"If I lose, I'll call the boss. Authority always has another office behind it."
"Let's go, go og. No, go. See? Even command language slips when the record gets nervous."
"Excuse me. I meant to sound ready."
"I'm in a foul mood because the Mayor doesn't like me."
"This paper may take hours to sign. Delaying a record is the closest thing City Hall gives me to power."
"Aren't you Bosch? School holiday? Homework done? Teeth brushed? Bed dry?"
"Forgive me. Adults become checklists when they can't protect the child from the route."
"Mayor Pirkle, pleased to meet you. You beat the bullies, which lets peace take credit after the danger is over."
"A key to the shack? Fine. Take it. But if the record turns violent, remember: responsibility never entered this office."
"Next election, support Mayor Pirkle."
"A speech sounds more sincere when someone else writes the need before you write the words."
"Thank you for your help."
"That's the official sentence. The unofficial one is shorter: thank you for making the file less embarrassing."
"City Hall is east. Where the sun rises, where the paper warms up first."
"Follow the light if you trust light to tell you what it has already touched."
"Trouble creates a market for hints."
"I can sell you a direction, Bosch. I cannot sell you the part where the direction stops selling you back."
"Lier X. Agerate finally found something interesting."
"He says he'll only show it to you, which means the treasure already knows what name to ask for."
"I wonder if EarthBound has been released yet."
"Sometimes a town waits for its own source material to arrive and explain why everyone sounds borrowed."
A mother mouse presents her son like a small, living exit clause.
"Take him if you get lost. He knows the way out because mazes underestimate anything they cannot file as a person."
The mouse stands at the edge of the dark road and asks for faith without paperwork.
"Trust me there. Some routes only become visible after the record gives up watching."
"O o o o. Do I come too?"
"I can be small, quiet, and very hard for a system to explain."
"I live rent-free with the Exit mice. They are kind, fast, and not impressed by property language."
"When a maze claims you, they treat the claim like a clerical error."
"I read Our Society is Convenient. Hotels and drugstores have phones; green costs a dollar, black is free."
"Convenience is just surveillance with instructions printed clearly enough to feel generous."
"Lately I sense UFOs overhead. Maybe I'm dreaming."
"Dreams are cheaper to dismiss than witnesses, so the town files most witnesses under dreams."
"Our Convenient Society says modern life demands an ATM in every hotel and drugstore."
"Imagine needing a book to notice the machines that already know when you're broke."
The person says nothing, which in Signal Town counts as a difficult kind of testimony.
"I'm cramming for tomorrow's big test: hotels refill HP and PP; life recovery restores HP, not PP."
"A test about games is still a test about obedience. Memorize the rules before the rules memorize you."
"I'm researching Giant Step, north and west of Signal Town. They say it has special power."
"Places get powerful when enough people point at them but not enough people ask who benefits."
"I saw a Magic butterfly outside the window. Not in the encyclopedia. There, in the air."
"Get close and your cares fly away. For a moment, the record can't keep up with anything that light."
"Who are you here to see? The patient was just brought in, still unconscious."
"Pay the doctor's fee and we'll keep the body on file until the person returns to it."
"You may not believe it, but I'm a healer."
"The hospital handles ordinary damage. I handle the conditions too embarrassing for official medicine to name cleanly."
"Diamondized. Mushroomized. Conditions the hospital can pronounce but not forgive."
"We wait in lobbies because shame always arrives before the patient does."
"I can examine each of you for a fee."
"The body becomes a line item; the line item becomes proof that somebody is still inside."
"Around here, our doctor is number one."
"That's what the lobby says, and lobbies are built to make waiting sound like confidence."
"I was SMAAAAASHED by a mouse."
"Small things hit hardest when the record has already decided they don't count."
"Here to visit my daughter? So you're the one who calls at night and talks for hours."
"No? Then why did your version come through the line before you came through the door?"
"I came to visit a friend, slipped in the hallway, and became a patient too."
"That's the tragic version. The civic version calls it efficient intake."
"You've grown since I last saw you. You're beginning to look like a man."
"Or like the town's idea of one. I like the harder version better, the one still deciding."
"I dreamed you were traveling with a cute girl."
"If the dream becomes record, say hi for me. Single people learn to flirt with possible futures."
"I'll give you my cap. I know you've wanted it for a long time."
"Wear something with a history. The route hates when an object remembers who touched it."
"I've been waiting for you. Help make the world blue, happy, peaceful, simple enough to process."
"Be my right hand. A right hand signs faster when the head stops asking who wrote the form."
"Aai i i i e e e e e e!"
"I screamed because nobody had issued me a better response yet."
"Blue, blue. Want to be one of us? Stop what you're doing and meet Mr. Carpainter."
"He loves a claimant who arrives pre-flattened."
"Blue, blue. Am I blocking you?"
"I'll move. Even devotion has to make room when the route has a stronger appointment."
"Blue, blue. Don't bother us. We're in the middle of prayer."
"Fine, I'll move. The line to heaven is still a line."
"Blue, blue. You think I can only walk in place?"
"Watch closely. Even a loop can perform dissent if someone files the footage wrong."
"Green, green. No, that's not it."
"I'm new. My mouth hasn't fully accepted the color policy."
"Blue, blue. I'll stand somewhere else."
"Belief is portable when the room needs rearranging."
"Blew, blew. I'm learning to whistle because whistling makes one smile."
"If happiness can be trained into the mouth, maybe nobody checks the eyes."
"Blue, blue. Arm the torpedoes, full speed ahead, or something official like that."
"Now I'll crush you. Violence sounds calmer when it borrows procedure."
"Blue, blue. Don't disrupt our meeting."
"A meeting is a crowd with minutes. A crowd with minutes can call stomping consensus."
"Blue, blue. Strange, unmasked fellow."
"Don't go to heaven. Heaven has an intake standard, and your face is still arguing with itself."
"Blue, blue. Anti-blue boy."
"You're getting on my nerves because you keep arriving in colors the doctrine can't compress."
"Blue, blue. Those who aren't believers must not be good."
"Eternity is where bad categories go when the village runs out of patience."
"Blue, blue. This should wake you up."
"The funny thing about forced awakening is how much it resembles being knocked unconscious."
"Blue, blue. Your outfit is wicked."
"Fabric confesses before people do. That's what the doctrine says when it gets lazy."
"Blue, blue. Why would a person who's not blue be here?"
"The question is a fence. You can hear how proud it is of being short."
"Blue, blue. You can't be happy unless you know Happy-Happyism."
"Happiness gets easier to manage after it becomes a membership requirement."
"When the world is changed to blue, a peaceful country can be established. Do you understand now?"
"Agreement is nicer when it arrives as comprehension. Disagreement can be filed as confusion."
"You might misunderstand me, but Carpainter's voice affects minds like a hypnotist."
"A warning becomes dangerous when it still has to speak inside the room it warns you about."
"We wanted to join the Happy-Happy group. Married couple from Twoson."
"Two people can become one file if they stand close enough and answer blue at the same time."
"This is the Happy-Happyism counseling office. Once you obtain happiness, visit headquarters."
"Counseling prepares the person. Headquarters prepares the version that will be easier to keep."
"I not know you. My kind are in valley over there."
"Bye bye. Outsiders always bring papers first and questions second."
"The refreshing herb says it helps colds, poison, depression, and the illnesses that won't heal cleanly."
"An herb with copy on the back is still a plant trying to survive marketing."
The receiver is lifted into a silence already shaped like Bosch.
"Someone has been waiting to hear whether your voice matches the one on file."
"We're one big happy family here. Officially, only family may stay."
"Study Happy-Happyism and we might rent you a bed. Refuse, and kinship becomes enforcement."
"Blue... blue. I can examine each of you for a fee."
"Even the body must tint itself before the village agrees to call it treatable."
"Blue... blue. Who are you here to see? The patient is unconscious."
"Pay the doctor and we'll keep the file breathing until the person catches up."
"I dreamt you and I were taking a walk."
"Then you arrived looking troubled, as if the dream sent its record ahead and kept the warmth."
"Late snack? No. I'm stuck on my project and wishing Dr. Andonuts were here."
"Take this bent key if you're going to the locker room. Useful things rarely stay straight after passing through school."
"If you're going outside, say hi to Maxwell. He watches out for younger students."
"Dangerous animals are wandering around, and the campus still calls that extracurricular."
"I'm so sleepy. Tony's birthday is tomorrow."
"I decorated every cookie by hand, so each one has proof it was loved before the party made love public."
"There's a monkey at the entrance. Buy bubble gum and you can have him for free; otherwise he's a dollar."
"Commerce has strange ideas about companionship, but the monkey seems to understand the terms."
"The bubble gum here is incredible."
"You should see the bubbles. For a second, even a mouth can make a world the record cannot enter."
The monkey gives one sharp answer and refuses the paperwork around it.
"Kyee kyee. Yeah."
The phone gives three small beeps, no greeting, no person.
"Beep-beep-beep. Some systems prefer refusal when recognition would cost too much."
"I'm the cook for the Tessie-Watching club. Stew?"
"Waiting for a monster is easier with a bowl in your hands. It gives the newspaper something domestic to crop out."
"If I find Tessie, do I get in the newspaper?"
"Fifteen minutes of fame is just a public version with a shorter lease."
The second phone repeats the same blank little alarm.
"Beep-beep-beep. A line can be dead and still sound busy."
"You finally got here. This is the third Sanctuary location, but it's mine now."
"Take it if you dare. Sacred places are only sacred until someone files possession first."
"We also run an inn. It's a night if you want to stay."
"No? Probably wise. The beds are old and dusty, and dust remembers every traveler better than we do."
The receiver clicks like a room pretending not to listen.
"Your voice enters first. The rest of you is always late to telecommunications."
"Small house, but stay the night."
"While you sleep, I can fix what's broken. Repair is the quiet art of returning an object to itself before morning files it as trash."
The receiver is already awake.
"Every call asks the same question: are you the source, or just the voice that arrived fastest?"
"Wait a minute, youngster. I can give you a hint for a price."
"If you decline, the system calls it confidence. If you pay, it calls the route proprietary."
The Bosch Terminal boots from a timestamp Bosch has not reached yet.
"Bosch, the route is not neutral. A public version is already moving through town ahead of your body."
"That version is the Malady: symptom, mask, and civic convenience with your smile stapled on."
"Milady is the engine underneath it. Rumor, paperwork, feed pressure, all one cheap machine."
"Walk the route anyway. Make the public version prove provenance in daylight."
MiFella hits the door like the algorithm owes him rent.
"Bosch. Up. You're trending before breakfast, which is legally the worst kind of trending."
"Some public version of you is already outside, polite and wrong. Town's eating it up."
"Bring shoes. Bring a face you can prove is yours. The trouble is making good time."
The Public Version Clique doesn't crowd you. It arranges itself around you, smiling, like a search result that already knows your name.
"Relax, Bosch. We did the hard part. There's a cleaner you, signed and ready. Just nod and let this one count."
"Refuse and you stop being a person and start being a dispute. ...Oh, you're going to dispute it. Adorable. Cataloged."
They step into the route like they own the receipt. Whatever you do next, they'll file as the fight starting.
